response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering skills and Linux expertise with 15+ years of experience, this role requires critical domain-specific qualifications that are absent: native-level Japanese proficiency (mandatory), deep embedded networking and security protocol expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption), and demonstrated Linux kernel modification experience. Recent career progression (last 3 years in full-stack web development) has moved away from embedded systems and security domains, suggesting a significant ramp-up would be required to meet role expectations.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience across diverse domains with proven ability to learn new technologies and language stacks
Demonstrated Linux expertise and embedded systems background (National Instruments, GISCollective), plus experience with low-level system components and infrastructure
Strong C/C++ background from National Instruments role writing system components, indicating foundational embedded systems capability
concerns: No native Japanese language proficiency despite being a hard requirement; resume shows English, Romanian, and basic German only
Lacks demonstrated expertise in required security technologies (encryption, key exchange, MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portals) and embedded networking protocols (Layer-2/3, NETCONF, SNMP)
Recent 3-year career trajectory heavily focused on web development (TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS) with no recent embedded networking, security protocols, or kernel-level work

key_factors.factor: General software engineering capability and Linux background
